Name: ActiveNet
LongDescription: ACTIVENet, offered by ACTIVE Network (a subsidiary of Global Payments), is a cloud-based Software-as-a-Service (SaaS) platform designed to help organizations—particularly parks & recreation departments, YMCAs, non-profits, schools, camps, and sports leagues—manage their programs, facilities, members, events, and payments.
LongDescriptionPlainText: ACTIVENet, offered by ACTIVE Network (a subsidiary of Global Payments), is a cloud-based Software-as-a-Service (SaaS) platform designed to help organizations—particularly parks & recreation departments, YMCAs, non-profits, schools, camps, and sports leagues—manage their programs, facilities, members, events, and payments.
PrimaryUseCase:
ActiveNet, developed by Active Network, is an advanced software solution designed to facilitate the management and administration of community activities, events, and recreational programs. It offers a comprehensive suite of features including program registration, facility reservation, member management, and online payment processing. The application is engineered to handle complex scheduling, automated notifications, and customized reporting, making it a versatile tool for streamlining operations in community centers, parks, schools, and other recreational facilities. Furthermore, ActiveNet's robust API capabilities provide seamless integration with third-party systems, enhancing its interoperability within a larger application ecosystem.

The application also includes sophisticated modules for customer relationship management (CRM), financial accounting, and marketing, enabling organizations to maintain detailed records, manage financial transactions, and conduct targeted outreach efforts. In terms of deployment, ActiveNet supports both cloud-based and on-premise implementations, catering to diverse organizational needs and IT infrastructures. It is important to note that ActiveNet is a standalone application developed by Active Network and is not part of a larger software suite. For further reference, visit the main website at www.activenetwork.com.

LongDescription:
Cartegraph OMS (Operations Management System) is an advanced software solution designed to help local governments and organizations efficiently manage their infrastructure assets, streamline workflows, and enhance decision-making through data-driven insights.

3- year contract.  new contract in 2026

3- year contract.  new contract in 2026
PrimaryUseCase:
Cartegraph OMS, designed by Cartegraph, is a sophisticated operations management system engineered to enhance the efficiency of municipal and utility asset management. The platform offers an integrated suite of features that include work order management, asset tracking, inventory management, and preventive maintenance scheduling. Leveraging geospatial data, Cartegraph OMS allows for detailed mapping and visualization of assets, enabling users to pinpoint asset locations and assess their conditions in real-time. The application supports comprehensive data capture and reporting capabilities, which facilitate data-driven decision-making processes for optimal resource allocation and operational efficiency.

The application is equipped with robust tools for asset lifecycle management, encompassing acquisition, utilization, and decommission phases. Additionally, Cartegraph OMS supports mobile access, allowing field personnel to update asset data and work orders in real-time, thereby enhancing field-to-office communication and reducing latency in data entry. This standalone application can function independently or be integrated with additional software solutions. Cartegraph, the developer of Cartegraph OMS, maintains a focus on providing advanced technology for public infrastructure management. For more information, the platform can be explored at the main website domain, cartegraph.com.

Embedded with advanced features like predictive analytics and trend analysis, it empowers stakeholders to anticipate future scenarios based on historical data, thus enhancing strategic planning and operational efficiencies. Part of the Cayenta suite developed by N. Harris Computer Corporation, this application is accessible through the main website domain: www.cayenta.com.

PrimaryUseCase:
Cityworks is an established enterprise asset management (EAM) and community development software application designed to support public infrastructure operations. Leveraging Esri’s ArcGIS platform, Cityworks integrates geographic information system (GIS) capabilities to provide robust tools for managing, tracking, and maintaining assets in a spatially aware context. The application enables users to manage work orders, service requests, inspections, and permitting from a unified interface that ties directly into spatial data. This facilitates optimized resource management and data-driven decision-making. Features such as real-time updates, mobile capabilities, and customizable workflows allow for streamlined operations and improved efficiency in asset lifecycle management.

In the realm of public asset management, Cityworks supports use cases ranging from utilities and transportation to parks and public works. Through its versatile modules, Cityworks can handle functions like asset inspections, preventive maintenance scheduling, and regulatory compliance tracking in various municipal and county government scenarios. The application's extensibility is reflected in its ability to integrate with other business systems and technologies, thereby providing a comprehensive solution for infrastructure management. Cityworks is part of the broader suite of solutions provided by Trimble Inc., a leader in transforming how the world works by delivering products and services that connect the physical and digital worlds. The main website for further information is www.cityworks.com.

Name: Eassist (Public Stuff)
LongDescription: The eAssist (Public Stuff) application is a digital platform that enables citizens to report non-emergency issues directly to local government agencies for efficient resolution and improved community services.
LongDescriptionPlainText: The eAssist (Public Stuff) application is a digital platform that enables citizens to report non-emergency issues directly to local government agencies for efficient resolution and improved community services.
PrimaryUseCase:
eAssist (Public Stuff) is a robust business application designed to facilitate efficient communication and service request management between citizens and municipal governments. The platform enables residents to report non-emergency issues such as potholes, broken streetlights, or graffiti through a web-based interface or mobile application. Utilizing an integrated GIS mapping system, eAssist accurately locates the reported issues and routes them directly to the appropriate municipal department for resolution. The application also features a tracking system that allows citizens to monitor the status of their requests and offers municipal employees a streamlined workflow for handling, updating, and closing service tickets.

Primarily envisioned for urban environments, eAssist includes capabilities for customer relationship management, project tracking, and data analytics, all aimed at improving urban management and citizen satisfaction. The application supports customizable fields and workflows, enabling municipalities to tailor the platform to their specific needs and operational procedures. Additionally, Public Stuff, the manufacturer, ensures that eAssist integrates seamlessly with existing municipal IT infrastructures and other third-party applications. eAssist can function as a standalone solution or as part of a larger suite of municipal management tools provided by Public Stuff. For more information, visit the main website at www.publicstuff.com.

LongDescription: Google Earth Pro is a geospatial software application that offers advanced features and tools for viewing, analyzing, and manipulating satellite imagery, maps, and geographic data on a 3D globe.
LongDescriptionPlainText: Google Earth Pro is a geospatial software application that offers advanced features and tools for viewing, analyzing, and manipulating satellite imagery, maps, and geographic data on a 3D globe.
PrimaryUseCase:
Google Earth Pro is a robust geospatial technology application developed by Google, designed for high-resolution satellite imagery and topographic data analysis. It facilitates advanced spatial data manipulation through features such as 3D modeling, historical imagery comparison, and complex polygonal measurement. The application allows users to import and export detailed geographical data in various formats including KML and SHP, enabling extensive customization and analytical workflows. The integration of demographic, parcel, and traffic data layers further supports in-depth regional analysis and planning activities. 

Potential use cases for Google Earth Pro span multiple domains including environmental science, urban planning, and resource management. Its capability to generate elevation profiles and visualize terrain at granular levels makes it essential for planning large-scale infrastructure projects. Educational institutions utilize it for instructive simulations and research, while organizations engaged in disaster response benefit from its real-time data updates and visualization tools. Google Earth Pro operates as a standalone application, distinct from other Google offerings, and can be accessed via the manufacturer's main website at [https://earth.google.com](https://earth.google.com).

Name: Key Watcher
LongDescription: Key Watcher is an advanced electronic key management system designed to securely store, track, and manage keys, providing real-time access control and audit capabilities.
LongDescriptionPlainText: Key Watcher is an advanced electronic key management system designed to securely store, track, and manage keys, providing real-time access control and audit capabilities.
PrimaryUseCase:
Key Watcher is an advanced electronic key management solution developed by Morse Watchmans, designed to streamline and secure key access within organizations. The system includes a sophisticated software interface that allows administrators to configure user-specific permissions, track key usage in real-time, and generate detailed audit reports. It integrates seamlessly with existing security infrastructures, offering features such as biometric authentication, proximity card access, and touch screen operation to ensure only authorized personnel can retrieve keys. Additionally, the application supports a variety of modules for expanded functionality, including asset lockers and weapon storage, making it highly adaptable to diverse operational needs.

The primary use cases for Key Watcher span across various sectors, including corporate enterprises, healthcare facilities, educational institutions, and government organizations where strict key control and accountability are paramount. Its capability to log key transactions, restrict access based on user roles, and alert administrators to unauthorized access attempts makes it a critical component in maintaining high levels of facility security and operational efficiency. Key Watcher is available as a standalone application and is not part of a broader software suite. Morse Watchmans, the manufacturer, provides detailed product information and support at their main website, www.morsewatchmans.com.

While offering extensive configurability to meet domain-specific requirements, Live Scan operates as a standalone application. The application is developed by CrossMatch Technologies. PrimaryUseCase:
OnBase, developed by Hyland Software, is a comprehensive enterprise content management (ECM) and case management system designed to streamline and automate business processes. With capabilities extending from document management to advanced data capture and workflow automation, OnBase provides a robust platform for managing a wide variety of content. The software enables secure storage, retrieval, and distribution of documents, while also offering tools for electronic forms, records management, and integration with other critical enterprise systems such as ERP, CRM, and email applications. Its integrated workflow engine facilitates the orchestration of complex, multi-step business processes, ensuring that the right information reaches the right individuals at the right time.

Potential use cases for OnBase include invoice processing, employee onboarding, contract management, and complaint resolution, among others. The system is highly configurable, allowing organizations to tailor workflows and content management capabilities to meet specific regulatory and operational requirements. Advanced features such as optical character recognition (OCR), intelligent data extraction, and robust analytics further enhance the efficiencies obtained through its deployment. OnBase is part of Hyland Software’s suite of enterprise management applications, which are often utilized in conjunction with other Hyland products to provide a comprehensive solution for managing an organization’s content and processes. For more information, visit the main website at www.hyland.com.

RedCap is a secure, web-based application designed for building and managing online surveys and databases to support data collection in research studies.
There is no cost for this application.  Used by Environmental Health.
PrimaryUseCase:
REDCap (Research Electronic Data Capture) is a secure, web-based application designed to support data collection and management for research studies. It provides a user-friendly interface for building and managing online surveys and databases, enabling the capture of both structured and unstructured data. Features include customizable data entry forms, real-time data validation, longitudinal study support, branching logic, and automated export capabilities to common statistical packages. The application includes audit trails for tracking data manipulation, user access controls for maintaining project confidentiality, and mechanisms for electronic consent collection.

REDCap is suitable for a range of use cases involving clinical trials, academic research data collection, registries, and observational studies. The platform supports data import/export, project calendar management, file uploading, and survey distribution via email or public links. It can be deployed in single or multi-site implementations and integrates with external systems through APIs. REDCap is a standalone application developed by Vanderbilt University. The main website is https://www.project-redcap.org.

Name: DeepFreeze
LongDescription: DeepFreeze is a system restore application that protects and preserves the original configuration of a computer by freezing its desired settings, allowing for easy restoration upon reboot while discarding any unwanted changes or modifications.
LongDescriptionPlainText: DeepFreeze is a system restore application that protects and preserves the original configuration of a computer by freezing its desired settings, allowing for easy restoration upon reboot while discarding any unwanted changes or modifications.
PrimaryUseCase:
DeepFreeze is a software application developed by Faronics, designed to protect and manage IT environments by freezing the desired state of a computer or server. Its primary feature is to lock down the configuration of a device so that any changes made to the system—whether through user activities or malicious software—are erased upon reboot. This is achieved by creating a snapshot of the preferred system configuration and preserving it, effectively wiping out all alterations that occur during usage sessions. As a result, DeepFreeze is commonly utilized in environments where system consistency is crucial, such as in educational institutions, libraries, and corporate settings where devices must be returned to a baseline state reliably. 

The application supports various platforms, including Windows and Mac systems, making it versatile for different organizational needs. DeepFreeze additionally offers customization options for administrators, allowing the ability to schedule maintenance windows, update processes, and licensing management centrally through its enterprise console. These capabilities enable IT departments to streamline their operations and reduce the time spent managing individual devices. While DeepFreeze can be deployed as part of an integrated IT management strategy, it functions effectively as a standalone solution. It serves as a foundational tool for maintaining operational integrity in multi-user computer environments. For further information about DeepFreeze and additional offerings from Faronics, the primary website domain is www.faronics.com.

LongDescription: The Bosch CDR (Crash Data Retrieval) Software application is a tool used to access and analyze crash data stored in a vehicle's Event Data Recorder (EDR), providing insights into the conditions and factors involved in a traffic incident.
LongDescriptionPlainText: The Bosch CDR (Crash Data Retrieval) Software application is a tool used to access and analyze crash data stored in a vehicle's Event Data Recorder (EDR), providing insights into the conditions and factors involved in a traffic incident.
PrimaryUseCase: The Bosch Crash Data Retrieval (CDR) Software is a specialized application designed to interface with Event Data Recorders (EDRs) installed in most modern vehicles. Its primary utility lies in the extraction and interpretation of crucial crash data post-incident, which can be instrumental in accident reconstruction and automotive research. The software facilitates access to pertinent vehicle data, such as pre-crash vehicle speed, throttle positions, brake and steering inputs, and other critical crash metrics captured at the time of the event. Equipped with detailed data reporting capabilities, Bosch CDR Software allows for a comprehensive data analysis by converting raw binary data into human-readable formats, thus ensuring that technical personnel can conduct an in-depth investigation with precision. The application supports a wide array of vehicle makes and models, reflecting its extensive compatibility with various automotive EDR systems. Users can expect robust data import and export functions, as well as comprehensive reporting tools that generate thorough, easy-to-understand crash data reports. Bosch CDR Software operates as a standalone application, representing Bosch's commitment to advancing vehicle safety through data-driven insights.

Enterprise licensing.

Enterprise licensing.
PrimaryUseCase:
GetInSync is a SaaS business application developed by Allstar Technologies Canada Limited, purposed for managing and documenting enterprise application portfolios. The platform excels in data organization within dimensions such as technical infrastructure, business processes, lifecycle status, cost attribution, inter-application data dependencies, and stakeholder ownership. Core functionalities include a dual-axis scoring model for business and technical criteria, enabling strategic visualizations like bubble charts. These tools facilitate comprehensive insight into technical and operational factors influencing application portfolios. The architecture of GetInSync features robust data fields for criticality, project impact during application in-flight stages, and both downstream and upstream dependencies, along with links to associated technical components to support a holistic portfolio overview.

This application leverages the TIMED framework—Tolerate, Invest, Migrate, Eliminate, Discover—for systematic asset classification, fostering implicit strategic development through rich metadata aggregation. It further provides meticulous mechanisms for stakeholder mapping, application data catalogue maintenance, and assessments of service impacts. The output module of GetInSync is capable of generating well-structured Word documents summarizing application specifics or strategic value plans, utilizing integrated connections and manual entries. It operates independently as a standalone application, not associated with a software suite. The manufacturer's main web presence is located at https://getinsync.ca.

LongDescriptionPlainText: SolarWinds is an IT management software application that provides comprehensive solutions for network performance monitoring, system management, and security to help organizations optimize and secure their IT infrastructure.
PrimaryUseCase:
SolarWinds is a comprehensive network management tool primarily designed for IT professionals to monitor and manage their network infrastructures effectively. It facilitates real-time network monitoring, enabling IT teams to gain visibility into their network environments through features such as Network Performance Monitor (NPM), which aggregates data on performance metrics and network paths. Furthermore, SolarWinds offers a range of functionalities including network traffic analysis, configuration management, and wireless network monitoring. The tool supports extensive reporting capabilities and customizable alerts which can identify and resolve downtime or performance degradation swiftly, making it integral for ensuring operational continuity in complex network setups.

SolarWinds is manufactured by SolarWinds, Inc., and it is part of a widely acclaimed suite of IT management products that covers various aspects of IT operations, including security, database management, and application performance monitoring. The unified platform of SolarWinds products allows seamless integration across different IT workflows, aiding in enhanced IT operations management. Despite offering standalone functionalities, its core strength lies in its integration capabilities with other products within the SolarWinds suite, enabling the consolidation of IT management processes. Further information about SolarWinds and their products can be found at their main website: www.solarwinds.com.

Name: Red Hat Enterprise

LongDescriptionPlainText: Red Hat Enterprise Linux (RHEL) is a commercial open-source operating system designed for enterprise environments, providing a stable, secure, and high-performance platform for applications across physical, virtual, and cloud environments.
PrimaryUseCase: Red Hat Enterprise Linux (RHEL) is a robust enterprise-grade operating system designed to provide a stable, secure, and high-performance environment for a wide range of enterprise applications. Its core capabilities include virtualization, high availability, and advanced security features, which facilitate the seamless deployment, management, and scaling of enterprise applications across diverse computing environments, including on-premises, cloud, and hybrid infrastructures. RHEL supports a vast array of software and hardware platforms, enabling businesses to optimize their IT landscapes with a scalable and flexible OS framework. It also integrates critical tools and utilities intended for system administration, application development, and resource management, making it a preferred choice for mission-critical applications in data centers. As part of its framework, Red Hat Enterprise Linux offers extensive support for container orchestration and management, facilitating the deployment of applications in a containerized format via tools like Docker and Kubernetes. This capability is particularly aligned with modern application architectures involving microservices, allowing enterprises to leverage container platforms for enhancing application portability and operational efficiency. Additionally, with comprehensive lifecycle management and automated system updates, RHEL ensures that systems remain compliant and up-to-date with the latest security patches and software enhancements. Red Hat Enterprise Linux is a standalone offering from Red Hat, Inc. and is not bundled as part of a suite. More information about Red Hat Enterprise Linux can be found on the Red Hat website at www.redhat.com.

Name: EasyDMarc Enterprise
LongDescription: EasyDMarc Enterprise is a comprehensive email security platform designed to protect businesses from phishing, spoofing, and other email threats by implementing and managing DMARC, SPF, and DKIM protocols.
LongDescriptionPlainText: EasyDMarc Enterprise is a comprehensive email security platform designed to protect businesses from phishing, spoofing, and other email threats by implementing and managing DMARC, SPF, and DKIM protocols.
PrimaryUseCase:
EasyDMarc Enterprise is a robust application designed to enhance email security through the implementation and management of Domain-based Message Authentication, Reporting & Conformance (DMARC) policies. It enables organizations to efficiently configure, monitor, and enforce DMARC protocols, which are critical for mitigating email spoofing and phishing attempts. The application provides comprehensive dashboards for detailed reporting and analytics on email authentication compliance, allowing IT administrators to gain visibility into unauthorized email usage. Additionally, EasyDMarc Enterprise supports SPF (Sender Policy Framework) and DKIM (DomainKeys Identified Mail) setup, ensuring robust email authentication measures are securely and effectively implemented across the organization's domains.

The application's implementation extends to use cases such as safeguarding brand reputation by preventing domain spoofing and enhancing overall email deliverability. The EasyDMarc Enterprise tool is designed for scalability, handling enterprise-level email systems with rich integration capabilities for seamless operation within existing IT infrastructures. EasyDMarc Enterprise is a standalone application from the manufacturer, EasyDMARC. Additional information and resources about the application can be found at the main website domain URL: easydmarc.com.

Name: Celerra Trend Micro
LongDescription: Celerra Trend Micro is an integrated security solution that provides antivirus scanning and malware protection for files stored on EMC Celerra network-attached storage systems.
LongDescriptionPlainText: Celerra Trend Micro is an integrated security solution that provides antivirus scanning and malware protection for files stored on EMC Celerra network-attached storage systems.
PrimaryUseCase:
Celerra Trend Micro is an integrated application solution focused on the protection of networked storage environments, particularly those leveraging EMC Celerra unified storage systems. The application enables real-time scanning of files written to or modified within the storage platform, utilizing pattern-based detection to intercept and identify potential threats such as viruses, worms, and malware. Its architecture is designed to interoperate with the native file event notification and response capabilities of the underlying NAS, enabling automated enforcement of security policies at the storage layer. Administrators are provided with tools to configure scanning policies, manage threat event logs, and define periodic or on-access scanning schedules tailored to organizational risk profiles.

The system includes centralized update mechanisms for maintaining current threat signatures and employs quarantine management features for isolating infected files without disrupting business operations. Logging and reporting modules allow for detailed tracking of scan outcomes, incident responses, and overall security posture over time. Celerra Trend Micro is typically deployed as a component within the broader EMC storage management ecosystem and is not marketed as a standalone solution. The application is manufactured by Trend Micro Incorporated, and further information can be found at https://trendmicro.com.

LongDescriptionPlainText: City Telephone 911 Location Services is an application that enables accurate identification and transmission of caller location information during emergency 911 calls to facilitate rapid and effective response by emergency services.
PrimaryUseCase:
City Telephone 911 Location Services is an enterprise-grade application designed to facilitate the accurate capture, management, and transmission of caller location data for emergency communications. The platform integrates with telephony infrastructure, extracting geospatial coordinates and civic addresses in real time as part of a Public Safety Answering Point (PSAP) workflow. Core features include automated querying of multiple location databases, normalization of varying address formats, and application of geo-fencing algorithms to improve precision in ambiguous cases. The solution supports standards-based interfaces, enabling interoperability with both legacy and next-generation 911 (NG911) systems.

The application additionally provides auditing and compliance modules, logging each transaction for forensic analysis and regulatory review. Access control mechanisms allow for role-based permissions across system operators, dispatchers, and IT administrators. Supported deployment models include on-premises, hybrid, and cloud-based environments, providing the architecture required to meet a range of jurisdictional requirements. City Telephone 911 Location Services operates as part of a broader suite of public safety solutions. The manufacturer of the application is City Telephone Systems Inc., and their main website is https://citytelephone.com.

LongDescriptionPlainText: LPI-PDF Scout is a software tool that automates the scanning, extraction, and analysis of data from PDF documents to streamline business processes and improve data accuracy.
PrimaryUseCase:
LPI-PDF Scout is an enterprise-grade application developed by Logical Progression Inc. that facilitates the automated discovery, parsing, and extraction of metadata and content from PDF documents at scale. The platform supports batch ingestion workflows, enabling organizations to process large volumes of PDFs for indexing, text extraction, and structure recognition. It employs customizable rule sets and pattern recognition capabilities to classify documents, extract specific data fields, and identify document variants, supporting integration with third-party systems through standardized APIs. The application also features audit logging, configurable access control, and export mechanisms that deliver processed results in common data formats.

Designed for use in document management, archival, and compliance-driven environments, LPI-PDF Scout’s feature set includes scheduled jobs, multi-source connectivity (including email, file shares, and cloud storage), and operational dashboards for process monitoring. The application is tuned to handle high-complexity documents, such as forms and contracts, with support for both textual and scanned image-based PDFs using embedded OCR capabilities. LPI-PDF Scout is offered as a standalone application and is not positioned as part of a broader product suite. The manufacturer’s main website is https://logicalprogression.ca.

Name: Phisher Plus
LongDescription: Phisher Plus is a cloud-based security solution designed to simulate phishing attacks, assess user susceptibility, and deliver targeted security awareness training to reduce organizational risk.
LongDescriptionPlainText: Phisher Plus is a cloud-based security solution designed to simulate phishing attacks, assess user susceptibility, and deliver targeted security awareness training to reduce organizational risk.
PrimaryUseCase:
Phisher Plus is an enterprise-grade application engineered to facilitate the simulation, tracking, and analysis of phishing threats within organizational environments. The platform enables administrators to design customized phishing campaigns, deploy simulated emails to targeted employee groups, and monitor response metrics such as click rates, credential submissions, and reporting behaviors. Phisher Plus integrates with common corporate email systems to ensure seamless campaign execution and provides administrators with tools for scenario creation, user segmentation, scheduling, and automated reminders. The application also supports fine-grained user access controls, robust audit logging, and multi-tiered reporting, allowing organizations to capture and review longitudinal threat exposure trends at both the individual and departmental levels.

The system features a comprehensive dashboard for tracking campaign outcomes, exporting raw event data for further analysis, and scheduling recurring assessments to gauge susceptibility over time. Phisher Plus supports integration with directory services for streamlined user provisioning and enables the assignment of follow-up training modules based on individual performance within phishing simulations. The application offers API endpoints for data extraction and can be configured to trigger alerts or policy workflows in incident management platforms. Phisher Plus is deployed as a standalone solution and is not included as part of a broader product suite. The application is manufactured by CyberDefend Technologies, and further information is available at https://cyberdefend.com.

Name: Powerbuilder Cloud Pro
LongDescription: PowerBuilder CloudPro is a development platform that enables organizations to modernize, deploy, and manage PowerBuilder applications in cloud environments with enhanced scalability and security.
LongDescriptionPlainText: PowerBuilder CloudPro is a development platform that enables organizations to modernize, deploy, and manage PowerBuilder applications in cloud environments with enhanced scalability and security.
PrimaryUseCase:
PowerBuilder CloudPro is a modern application development platform designed to facilitate the migration, modernization, and deployment of business applications to cloud environments. It provides a robust integrated development environment (IDE) that supports rapid application development using PowerScript, a proprietary object-oriented language. The platform enables developers to refactor legacy client/server PowerBuilder applications into cloud-ready, web-based architectures, leveraging the .NET framework for logic and deployment. CloudPro incorporates code converters, migration utilities, and support for RESTful web APIs, enabling the decoupling of application layers and integration with contemporary cloud-native services.

In addition to cloud migration features, PowerBuilder CloudPro supports cross-platform deployment, containerization, and orchestration using Docker and Kubernetes. The platform includes tools for application debugging, code profiling, and integration with source control systems such as Git. Data access is managed through support for ODBC, JDBC, and direct database interfaces, facilitating connectivity to major relational database systems. PowerBuilder CloudPro is offered as part of the Appeon product suite, which centers on enterprise application modernization and cloud transformation tools. The manufacturer is Appeon Inc., and the primary website domain is https://www.appeon.com.

LongDescription: SafetyCom Outdoor Warning Sirens is a system that manages, monitors, and activates outdoor warning sirens to provide timely alerts and notifications for public safety during emergencies.
LongDescriptionPlainText: SafetyCom Outdoor Warning Sirens is a system that manages, monitors, and activates outdoor warning sirens to provide timely alerts and notifications for public safety during emergencies.
PrimaryUseCase:
SafetyCom Outdoor Warning Sirens is a specialized business application designed for the centralized management, monitoring, and activation of outdoor warning siren systems. The platform provides operators with a real-time dashboard for visualizing the geographic placement and operational status of installed siren devices. Core capabilities include remote control of siren activation, scheduling of test routines, logging of activation history, and alerting for system anomalies or faults detected within the siren network. Integration with geographic information systems (GIS) enables precise mapping of warning coverage areas and facilitates targeted activations based on situational parameters such as severe weather events or public safety incidents.

The application supports multi-level access controls, allowing different user roles to initiate alerts, manage device inventories, and review event histories. Automated notification workflows can be configured for escalation to supervisors or local authorities upon specific trigger conditions. SafetyCom Outdoor Warning Sirens’s architecture accommodates API-based integrations with external public safety or emergency management systems, enabling cross-system event synchronization and interoperability with municipal communication infrastructure. SafetyCom Outdoor Warning Sirens is marketed as a standalone application and is not part of a broader suite. The manufacturer is Veridian Solutions Inc., and the main website is https://veridiansolutions.com.

Its extensibility includes options for custom report generation scripts and the ability to design bespoke log processing rules. Name: Tenable Nessus
LongDescription: Tenable Nessus is a vulnerability assessment tool that scans and identifies security weaknesses, misconfigurations, and compliance issues across IT environments to help organizations manage and mitigate cybersecurity risks.
LongDescriptionPlainText: Tenable Nessus is a vulnerability assessment tool that scans and identifies security weaknesses, misconfigurations, and compliance issues across IT environments to help organizations manage and mitigate cybersecurity risks.
PrimaryUseCase:
Tenable Nessus is a vulnerability assessment solution designed to systematically identify, categorize, and report on security vulnerabilities within IT environments. The application performs active network scanning to detect a wide array of vulnerabilities, including missing patches, misconfigurations, and compliance violations across operating systems, network devices, databases, and applications. Nessus offers automated scanning workflows, policy-based assessments, and customizable scan templates, enabling organizations to tailor vulnerability management to their specific infrastructure and regulatory context. It supports integration with asset and patch management systems through available APIs, facilitating streamlined workflows for remediation tracking and risk prioritization.

The application includes frequent plugin updates that allow the scan engine to detect newly discovered vulnerabilities and apply the most recent assessment logic. Nessus produces detailed vulnerability reports with contextual information such as severity ratings, remediation guidance, and exploit availability, which can be exported in multiple formats including CSV, PDF, and HTML. Its scalable architecture supports both on-demand and scheduled scanning, and it can be deployed on-premises or in cloud environments. Tenable Nessus operates as a standalone application within the broader Tenable product ecosystem but is not inherently bundled as part of a suite. The manufacturer of Tenable Nessus is Tenable, Inc., and the main website domain is https://tenable.com.

ZenGRC is a governance, risk, and compliance (GRC) management application developed by Reciprocity, Inc. The platform enables organizations to centralize and automate key GRC activities, including the documentation and tracking of controls, audit workflows, and risk assessments. ZenGRC provides configurable frameworks with support for multiple regulatory standards such as SOX, ISO 27001, NIST, and GDPR, allowing users to structure policies, procedures, and evidence collection within a unified interface. Its workflow engine facilitates scheduling, management, and reporting of control testing and remediation tasks, while advanced role permissions enable granular assignment of responsibilities across teams. Integrated dashboards and reporting capabilities allow users to visualize control status, audit findings, and risk postures.

Interoperability with external systems is supported through API integrations and data import/export features, enabling linkage with other business processes and tools within the organization. ZenGRC accommodates risk and issue registers with customizable fields, linking these to specific assets, processes, and compliance requirements. The application supports document version management, automated notifications, and history tracking of compliance activities. ZenGRC is marketed as a standalone SaaS application, not as part of a broader suite. The manufacturer is Reciprocity, Inc., and the main website is https://reciprocity.com.
